mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero, the persistent zero-lower-bound stance continues to suppress the equity discount rate, supporting a sustained upward drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS < 0.25% are not statistically greater than unconditional mean returns (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market healing that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.5% down from prior-year peak levels above 8%
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 20-day return win rate <= 50% or Rank IC < 0.02 across walk-forward windows spanning at least 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales growth) and HOUST (housing starts growth) indicates broad-based consumer and housing demand expansion that is not captured by either series alone; this synchronized demand impulse drives S&P 500 forward gains over one month as cyclical sectors re-rate upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1036k units continuing post-recession recovery
regime_hint: synchronized housing and consumer demand upswing
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances underperform single-series RSAFS-only signal on Rank IC, or fail to exceed 55% positive-return hit rate over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession industrial recovery regime, sustained month-over-month gains in INDPRO signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that feeds into corporate earnings growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is strongest during early-to-mid cycle recoveries and weakens or inverts in late-cycle overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.74 rising from post-2009 trough
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict 60-day forward returns with Rank IC >= 0.03 during recovery-labeled subperiods, or effect direction flips sign in more than one out of three tested recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase exceeding an annualized 3% pace signals emerging inflation risk that raises expectations of future Fed tightening, causing a short-term S&P 500 pullback over the next trading week as markets price in tighter future policy.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 232.34 with low observed inflation as of decision date
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered CPI surprises are not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or triggers occur with negative hit rate <= 50% over available sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued private-sector hiring momentum that supports aggregate income and consumption, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over one month regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135474k persons showing multi-year uptrend
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS trailing growth and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment where FEDFUNDS remains near zero, rising HOUST housing starts signal amplified rate-sensitive demand that disproportionately boosts homebuilder and financial sector earnings, driving S&P 500 gains over a quarterly horizon; this effect is muted or reversed once FEDFUNDS rises materially above 2%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% alongside HOUST recovery to 1036k units
regime_hint: near-zero interest rate regime with housing recovery
failure_condition: HOUST-based 60-day predictive Rank IC does not exceed 0.03 while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or the effect fails to weaken (does not decay toward zero or flip) once tested against periods with FEDFUNDS > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A joint short-term surge in INDPRO and PAYEMS growth captures a synchronized supply-and-labor expansion signal that is more informative than either series alone for near-term risk appetite, producing a positive S&P 500 return burst over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 5-day forward return hit rate does not exceed the hit rate of either single-series trigger by at least 5 percentage points, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).
